title: PNG's answer to the deepfake problem
gist: With PNG's 2027 elections at risk of repeating a violent pattern seen in the prior campaign, a parliamentary inquiry recommended self-regulation and literacy measures for PNG media, but the more consequential unaddressed proposal is a purpose-built AI-based deepfake/disinformation detection tool co-designed by the Media Council of PNG with UNSW and UTS researchers that has received little policymaker attention.

themes.theme: PNG's disinformation vulnerability ahead of 2027 elections

insights.claim: Leading deepfake expert Hany Farid, a UC Berkeley professor, said in a New York Times report that he no longer trusts his own eyes, describing himself as 'going blind' because AI has evolved so fast it obscures truth and distorts reality — even for a top forensic expert.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: Signals that deepfake detection has outpaced even world-class human forensic expertise, raising the bar for what automated tools need to achieve, especially in low-resource media environments like PNG.
insights.locator: The New York Times recently reported the world's leading deepfake expert Hany Farid

insights.claim: Almost 40% of PNG's roughly 11 million population lives in poverty, lacks access to basic services or formal employment, is poorly educated, and has very low media literacy, yet millions have Facebook access via mobile phones, leaving them highly exposed to disinformation while being beyond the reach of mainstream journalism's accuracy standards.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: A structural mismatch between mobile-social-media penetration and media literacy/institutional capacity is a key election-violence risk factor to monitor in PNG through 2027.

insights.claim: PNG's previous national election campaign triggered a pattern of violence that resulted in more than 400 deaths and displacement of about 20,000 residents, and there is concern this could recur in the 2027 elections amid rising mis/disinformation risk.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: Concrete casualty/displacement baseline for assessing PNG political-violence risk heading into the 2027 election cycle.
insights.locator: it prepares for national elections in 2027

insights.claim: A March 2026 PNG parliamentary committee inquiry into journalism standards made 40 recommendations, with 20 endorsing the self-regulating role of the volunteer-run Media Council of PNG, including public funding for the Council, a national disinformation taskforce, industry-wide fact-checking protocols, a national digital/media literacy strategy, a Freedom of Press Act, and Criminal Code amendments protecting at-risk media workers.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: Tracks a concrete PNG legislative/regulatory pipeline (Freedom of Press Act, funded Media Council, disinformation taskforce) worth monitoring for implementation before the 2027 election.
insights.locator: In March 2026, a parliamentary committee report in PNG

insights.claim: Even if fully implemented, the inquiry's self-regulation and literacy recommendations would still be insufficient because organisations lack capacity for systematic, time-consuming verification work, and fact-checking often only occurs after suspicious content has already spread in the community — meaning training alone offers only a partial remedy.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: Signals that policy responses focused on media self-regulation without a technical detection layer will structurally fail to keep pace with disinformation spread — relevant to any vendor or funder assessing PNG media-integrity investment.
insights.locator: But they would still be insufficient

insights.claim: The Media Council of PNG, together with computer science researchers from UNSW and UTS, has proposed an automated fake news detector purpose-trained for the PNG civic/media context that gives near-real-time alerts on suspicious multimodal content (text, image, video, synthetic audio), provides bilingual English/Tok Pisin explanations, and risk-scores content to distinguish normal political exaggeration from high-risk deepfakes — but this proposal has received scant policymaker attention.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: A concrete, tested, low-attention AI tool proposal for a high-risk 2027 election — worth tracking for funding/deployment decisions as a leading indicator of PNG election-integrity infrastructure.
insights.locator: With an eye to the 2027 PNG elections

insights.claim: The proposed detector combines a lightweight machine-learning model for rapid screening with a complementary large language model for contextual reasoning and explanation, and is designed for a specialised rather than universal purpose specifically to minimise the factual errors that still affect general-purpose LLMs.
insights.speaker: Geoff Heriot
insights.so_what: A specific technical architecture pattern (narrow ML detector + LLM explainer) for disinformation tooling in low-resource markets, relevant to anyone evaluating similar civic-tech deployments elsewhere.
insights.locator: The tool would combine a lightweight machine-learning detector
